# Reference results captured with systemd-analyze 261 (systemd 261.1).
#
# Each case was run as:
#   systemd-analyze calendar --base-time="BASE" "EXPRESSION"
#
# Only deterministic fields are retained. CI does not invoke systemd; these
# cases document the compatibility oracle used by tests/test_timers.nim.

[amsterdam]
base=2026-01-15 00:00:00 UTC
expression=*-*-* 02:00:00 Europe/Amsterdam
normalized=*-*-* 02:00:00 Europe/Amsterdam
nextUtc=2026-01-15 01:00:00 UTC

[fractional-rounding]
base=2026-01-15 12:34:00 UTC
expression=*-*-* *:*:00.9999995 UTC
normalized=*-*-* *:*:01 UTC
nextUtc=2026-01-15 12:34:01 UTC

[third-last-day]
base=2026-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
expression=*-02~03 00:00:00 UTC
normalized=*-02~03 00:00:00 UTC
nextUtc=2026-02-26 00:00:00 UTC

[weekday-and-date]
base=2026-07-31 23:00:00 UTC
expression=Mon..Fri *-*-01 02:00:00 UTC
normalized=Mon..Fri *-*-01 02:00:00 UTC
nextUtc=2026-09-01 02:00:00 UTC

[spring-gap]
base=2026-03-29 00:00:00 UTC
expression=*-*-* 02:30:00 Europe/Amsterdam
normalized=*-*-* 02:30:00 Europe/Amsterdam
nextUtc=2026-03-30 00:30:00 UTC

[fall-overlap]
base=2026-10-24 23:00:00 UTC
expression=*-*-* 02:30:00 Europe/Amsterdam
normalized=*-*-* 02:30:00 Europe/Amsterdam
nextUtc=2026-10-25 00:30:00 UTC
